title: Machu Picchu
html:
Machu Picchu

The canonical "finesse-the-route" datapoint in Yahir's travel ledger. Yahir bypassed the conventional ticketed Machu Picchu entry and walked ~25 miles total along the train tracks to the citadel (gloss, prep note). Becomes the prototype for every later off-trail move (petra, ABC trek).

Chronology

  • 2021-07-28 — 2021 07 28 vegetarian drive todos — earliest prep mention
  • 2021-08-21 — 2021 08 21 pre peru packing list — packing focused on the hike
  • 2021-08-26 — 2021 08 26 bus ride to cusco — Lima→Cusco 20+ hour bus, the route to MP
  • 2021-08-31 — 2021 08 31 seize the day machu picchu prep — the canonical prep note, "seize the day" framing
  • 2021-08-03 — 2021 08 03 kira ford mentor intro — mentor-narrative framing
  • 2021-07-29 — 2021 07 29 LIMA CUSCO — Lima/Cusco logistics
  • 2023-08-14 — 2023 08 14 Route from Cairo to Jordan — referenced as precedent for the Egypt→Jordan off-trail planning
  • 2024-03-02 — 2024 03 02 Questlist — added to the questlist as repeat-worthy
  • 2024-08-14 — 2024 08 14 travel occurrences — retrospective gloss naming the 25-mile walk
  • 2025-07-12 — 2025 07 12 good traits — Machu Picchu cited as evidence of a personality trait

Why it matters

Machu Picchu is the first vault-documented instance of Yahir's signature travel posture: if the official route costs money or constrains the experience, walk in from the side instead. The framing carries forward to:

  • Petra — off the marked path with emilio, scaled a mountain on descent
  • Annapurna Base Camp — full multi-day trek instead of helicopter package
  • Shanghai — chose the live-with-Chinese-students dorm instead of the CIEE bubble

The walk is the action-shape of voluntary adversity in travel form.

Connections
